title: cassia fasciculata var. rostrata
intro: Cassia fasciculata var. rostrata, also known as prairie fire, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its yellow flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant's roots, stems, and leaves have been employed for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cassia fasciculata var. rostrata are said to include treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with its herbal preparations often used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
